seoTitle: Create a Hanging Indent in Canva
metaDescription: Learn how to create a hanging indent in Canva for citations, lists, and other text that needs a clean, professional format.
intro: A hanging indent adds clarity and structure to text, especially useful for citation lists or formal documents. This guide covers how to create one inside Canva's text editor.

steps.heading: Understanding hanging indents
steps.body: A hanging indent keeps the first line of a paragraph flush left while indenting subsequent lines, commonly used in citations.

steps.heading: Step-by-step guide to creating hanging indents
steps.body: Select your text box in Canva and use spacing or paragraph settings to manually create the indent effect.

steps.heading: Practical applications in different fields
steps.body: Hanging indents are useful in academic citation lists, business proposals, and any document needing clear text hierarchy.

steps.heading: Tips for maximizing aesthetic quality
steps.body: Keep spacing consistent across all indented lines so the formatting looks intentional and polished.

faq.question: Does Canva have a built-in hanging indent button?
faq.answer: Canva doesn't always offer a dedicated one-click hanging indent option, so it may require manually adjusting spacing or tabs.

faq.question: Why are hanging indents important for citations?
faq.answer: They create visual separation between citation entries, making reference lists easier to scan and read.

faq.question: Can I apply this formatting to an entire document at once?
faq.answer: You'll generally need to apply the formatting to each relevant text block individually within Canva.

faq.question: Is this useful outside of academic writing?
faq.answer: Yes, hanging indents can also add a professional touch to proposals, reports, and certain marketing materials.
